IELTS requires 4 skills: Listening, Reading, Writing, and Speaking. Practice daily for 3 months to see major improvement.
Canada's Express Entry system gives bonus points for a job offer. Research your target province's PNP programs.
Germany offers tuition-free education at public universities for international students. Apply early — deadlines are in January.
A strong SOP (Statement of Purpose) explains your 'why': why this course, why this university, why now.
Australia's Post-Study Work Visa (TSS) lets you work for 2–4 years after graduation depending on your location.
Your GPA matters, but a compelling research statement can compensate for a slightly lower score at many universities.
Education loans in India are available up to ₹1.5 crore for top global universities. Collateral is optional for amounts under ₹7.5L.
UK student visas require proof of funds for the first year. Keep your bank statement ready 28 days before applying.
LinkedIn optimization is crucial. Alumni connections from your target university are your best interview network.
Apply to at least 10 universities: 2 dream, 5 target, 3 safety schools. Diversify your applications.
The GRE General Test is accepted by thousands of programs. A 320+ score significantly boosts your admit chances.
Canada's PGWP (Post-Graduation Work Permit) duration matches your study duration, up to 3 years.
Australia's cost of living varies widely. Melbourne and Sydney are expensive; Canberra and Adelaide are much more affordable.
Germany requires blocked account of ~€11,208 for your visa. Start setting this up 3 months before your visa appointment.
Mock interviews at your university's career center can increase job offer rates by 40%. Practice, practice, practice.
Your first 30 days in a new country matter most. Connect with the international student office the day you arrive.
ROI for an MS in Computer Science in the USA averages 300%+ over 10 years. The investment pays off.
Income-sharing agreements (ISAs) are an emerging alternative to education loans. Research ISA programs in your target country.
Most universities now accept DUOLINGO English Test as an alternative to IELTS. Check your target university's policy.
Start your visa application at least 3 months before your intake date. Delays are common.
Always have a plan B university. Rejections happen even to perfect candidates. Stay resilient.
Networking events on campus are as valuable as your degree. Attend every department seminar you can in year 1.
Canada's student work permit allows 20 hours/week during semester. Plan your budget around potential part-time income.
GMAT Focus Edition launched in 2023. It's shorter and more adaptive than the older GMAT. 645+ puts you in top 25% globally.
Australia's OSHC (Overseas Student Health Cover) is mandatory. Budget AUD 550–600 per year for this.
The UK Graduate Route visa allows 2 years (3 for PhD) of post-study work without a job offer. Very competitive option.
Research rankings matter differently by field. QS by Subject gives more accurate rankings than overall world rankings.
SOP tip: Use the 'Problem-Solution-Impact' framework. What problem drives you, what solution you're pursuing, and what impact you'll create.
Your first year abroad is the hardest. Set a monthly budget, find a community, and don't hesitate to ask for help.
Consider part-time campus jobs at your university. They provide income, experience, and crucial local networking.
